payload.title: Financial and Ridership Reports – March 2025
payload.description: MTA New York City Transit, Staten Island Railway, and Bus Company financial and ridership reports for March 2025, including budget performance, revenues, expenses, staffing, overtime, and ridership metrics.

warnings: source.publisher is relation context, not a closed enum or identity key. Publisher has 39 accepted/canonical observations and should link to entity context when useful. Suggested relation: published_by.

NYCT farebox revenue March 2025

evidence_refs.source_quote: NYCT farebox revenue totaled $312.0 million, which was $10.8 million (3.6%) higher than the Budget.

payload.claim_text: March YTD total headcount was 47,335, 2,684 lower than Budget. Non-reimbursable positions lower by 1,725, Reimbursable positions lower by 959.
payload.description: NYCT staffing vacancy breakdown by category

evidence_refs.source_quote: Non-reimbursable positions were lower by 1,725, and Reimbursable positions were lower by 959.

payload.claim_text: The largest vacancies were in Subways/Bus Maintenance (1,546 positions) and Subways/Buses Operations (715 positions). There were significant vacancies in Construction and Development (235 positions) and other administrative functions (256 positions).
payload.description: Areas with largest vacancies at NYCT

evidence_refs.source_quote: The largest vacancies were in Subways/Bus Maintenance (1,546 positions) and Subways/Buses Operations (715 positions).

payload.claim_text: Unfavorable non-reimbursable overtime was driven primarily by severe weather conditions, absentee coverage needs and vacancies. Unfavorable reimbursable results were due to the timing of capital project activity.
payload.description: Drivers of unfavorable overtime at NYCT

evidence_refs.source_quote: Unfavorable non-reimbursable overtime was driven primarily by severe weather conditions, absentee coverage needs and vacancies.

payload.claim_text: Total expenses of $25.395 million were $1.280 million (4.8%) lower than Budget. Primary drivers: timing of $2.010 million (39.1%) in non-labor expenses and lower labor costs of $0.983 million (5.7%) from vacant positions.
payload.description: Drivers of favorable expense variance at SIR

evidence_refs.source_quote: Total expenses of $25.395 million including non-cash liabilities were $1.280 million (4.8%) lower than the Budget. The primary drivers of this favorable variance were the timing of $2.010 million (39.1%) in non-labor expenses and lower labor costs of $0.983 million (5.7%) from the existence of vacant positions.

issues: evidence_refs[0].source_quote must be 280 characters or fewer

payload.claim_text: Total expenses of $25.395 million including non-cash liabilities were $1.280 million (4.8%) lower than the Budget. The primary drivers of this favorable variance were the timing of $2.010 million (39.1%) in non-labor expenses and lower labor costs of $0.983 million (5.7%) from the existence of vacant positions.
payload.description: Drivers of favorable expense variance at SIR

evidence_refs.source_quote: Total expenses of $25.395 million including non-cash liabilities were $1.280 million (4.8%) lower than the Budget.

payload.description: MTABC farebox revenue YTD March 2025

payload.description: MTABC farebox revenue March 2025

evidence_refs.source_quote: MTABC March 2025 farebox revenue of $17.4 million was $1.5 million (9.3%) above the Budget

payload.claim_text: Overtime was $65.7 million (58.3%) unfavorable primarily due to severe weather conditions and higher than projected absentee coverage needs and vacancies.
payload.description: Reason for unfavorable overtime variance at NYCT

evidence_refs.source_quote: Overtime was $65.7 million (58.3%) unfavorable primarily due to severe weather conditions and higher than projected absentee coverage needs and vacancies.

payload.claim_text: Paratransit Contracts was $25.7 million (18.1%) unfavorable primarily due to higher trip volume and support costs.
payload.description: NYCT paratransit contracts unfavorable variance

evidence_refs.source_quote: Paratransit Contracts was $25.7 million (18.1%) unfavorable primarily due to higher trip volume and support costs.

payload.claim_text: Financial results exclude $779.5 million of federal formula grant funding which was recorded as operating revenue that will be applied to PAVSD capital.
payload.description: Note about excluded federal formula grant funding

evidence_refs.source_quote: Financial results exclude $779.5 million of federal formula grant funding which was recorded as operating revenue that will be applied to PAVSD capital.

payload.claim_text: Total expenses of $242.5 million were $42.5 million (14.9%) lower than the Budget. Total expenses before non-cash liabilities of $224.8 million were below Budget by $12.1 million (5.1%) due to favorable labor costs of $4.7 million (2.6%) and non-labor expense underruns of $7.4 million (13.3%).
payload.description: MTA Bus Company favorable expense variance

evidence_refs.source_quote: Total expenses of $242.5 million were $42.5 million (14.9%) lower than the Budget.

payload.description: NYCT farebox revenue variance YTD March 2025 vs Budget

evidence_refs.source_quote: Farebox revenue was higher by $19.4 million (2.3%) with Subway farebox revenue higher by $17.0 million (2.5%) and Bus farebox revenue higher by $1.0 million (0.6%).

payload.gap_text: Results are based on the preliminary close of the general ledger and are subject to review and adjustment. Actuals do not include post-close adjustments, which will be captured in the subsequent month's YTD results.

evidence_refs.source_quote: Results are based on the preliminary close of the general ledger and are subject to review and adjustment. Please note actuals do not include post-close adjustments, which will be captured in the subsequent month's YTD results.
